description: Also known as 🕌 Tawhid - Divine Oneness.
name: Tawhid

keyMyths.title: The Declaration of Faith (Shahada)
keyMyths.description: The Shahada, "There is no god but God (Allah), Muhammad is the messenger of God," encapsulates the core of Tawhid. It asserts the absolute uniqueness and indivisibility of Allah, rejecting all forms of polytheism and associating partners with Him.

keyMyths.title: The Story of Abraham and the Idols
keyMyths.description: Abraham's rejection of idol worship and his destruction of the idols of his people is a powerful narrative illustrating Tawhid. He championed the worship of the one true God, facing persecution for his unwavering belief in divine oneness.

keyMyths.title: The Throne Verse (Ayat al-Kursi)
keyMyths.description: Ayat al-Kursi (Quran 2:255) describes Allah's absolute power, knowledge, and sovereignty over all creation. It emphasizes His self-sufficiency and the absence of any being comparable to Him, solidifying the concept of Tawhid.

keyMyths.title: The Story of the People of the Cave (Ashab al-Kahf)
keyMyths.description: The story of the People of the Cave, who fled persecution for their belief in one God, highlights the importance of upholding Tawhid even in the face of adversity. Their unwavering faith and reliance on Allah demonstrate the strength derived from belief in divine oneness.

keyMyths.title: The Divine Names (Asma al-Husna)
keyMyths.description: The 99 Names of Allah represent the various attributes and qualities of the one God, each reflecting a different aspect of His perfection. While numerous, they all point to the same singular, unique, and incomparable divine being, reinforcing Tawhid.

sources.name: The Quran
sources.description: The central religious text of Islam, considered by Muslims to be the word of God (Allah).
sources.title: The Quran

sources.name: Sahih al-Bukhari
sources.description: One of the most authentic and comprehensive collections of Hadith (sayings and actions of Prophet Muhammad).
sources.title: Sahih al-Bukhari

sources.name: Sahih Muslim
sources.description: Another highly regarded and authentic collection of Hadith.
sources.title: Sahih Muslim

sources.name: Tafsir al-Tabari
sources.description: A classical and influential commentary (Tafsir) on the Quran by Muhammad ibn Jarir al-Tabari.
sources.title: Tafsir al-Tabari

sources.name: Fazlur Rahman, Islam
sources.description: A modern scholarly work exploring various aspects of Islamic thought, including Tawhid.
sources.title: Fazlur Rahman, Islam
